zh.Preston, Lancashire, England, United Kin: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾普雷斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: Isabelle De Singleton於1274年出生在英國藍卡爾吉可哈姆。她於大約1295年與Gilbert de Singleton在英國藍卡爾吉可哈姆結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1340年在她的家鄉享年66歲。

zh-hans.Preston, Lancashire, England, United Kin: 英国英格兰藍卡爾普雷斯頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isabelle De Singleton于1274年出生在英国藍卡爾吉可哈姆。她于大约1295年与Gilbert de Singleton在英国藍卡爾吉可哈姆结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1340年在她的家乡享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: Isabelle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Singleton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Singleton (Lancashire), probably from Old English scingol ‘shingle, wooden roof tile’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Bearers of this surname are said to be descended from Ughtred de Sinleton, holder of lands in Amounderness wapentake (Lancashire) c. 1160–90. This name was first recorded in Ireland in 1308 and firmly established in Louth and Monaghan during the 17th century.
habitational name from Singleton (Sussex), probably from Old English sængel, sengel ‘bundle, tuft’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Shingleton
meanings: English: late medieval variant of   Singleton  .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Isabelle De Singleton was born in 1274, in Kirkham, Lancashire, England. She married Gilbert de Singleton about 1295, in Kirkham, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1340, in her hometown, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabelle De Singleton est née en 1274, à Kirkham, Lancashire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Gilbert de Singleton environ 1295, à Kirkham, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1340, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 66 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Isabelle De Singleton wurde 1274 in Kirkham, Lancashire, England geboren. Sie hat um 1295 in Kirkham, Lancashire, England Gilbert de Singleton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1340 im Alter von 66 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Isabelle De Singleton è nata il nel 1274 nel Kirkham, Lancashire, England. Lei sposato Gilbert de Singleton circa 1295, nel Kirkham, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1340, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 66 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabelle De Singleton(은)는 1274년 영국 랭카실르 커카에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1295년 영국 랭카실르 커카에서 Gilbert de Singleton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1340년 그녀의 고향에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Isabelle De Singleton nasceu em 1274, em Kirkham, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Gilbert de Singleton aproximadamente 1295, em Kirkham, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1340, em sua cidade natal, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabelle De Singleton родилась в 1274 г. в местности кирхам, ланкашир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gilbert de Singleton около 1295 г. в местности кирхам, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1340 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 66 лет.
